description:
# Why

bl-ffb4 introduced a second valid state-checkout layout —
`.balls/state-repo/` materialized by `state_repo::ensure` when
committed config sets `master_url` — completely separate from the
legacy `.balls/worktree/`. bl doctor's drift checks all predate this
and assume the legacy layout, so on a master_url-configured repo with
a missing or corrupt state-repo, doctor produces diagnostics that
point at the wrong path (or reports the absence of `.balls/worktree/`
as a problem when it's correctly absent).

Doctor is the 'named reason this repo can't run bl' tool. Wrong
diagnostics here defeat its only job. Now that bl-38dd has shipped, a
master_url repo's healthy state also includes a
`.balls/tasks -> state-repo/.balls/tasks` symlink, which doctor also
doesn't know to check.

# What needs to change

- doctor.rs reads `.balls/config.json` first and branches on
  `master_url` to decide which checkout to inspect
- the 'state worktree missing' diagnostic names the right path
  (`.balls/state-repo` when applicable) and the right fix
  (`bl prime` auto-provisions in master_url mode via
  `auto_provision_master`; legacy is `setup_state_branch`)
- new master_url-mode checks:
  - state-repo's `origin` URL matches committed master_url — a
    user-edited config diverging from the materialized clone is
    exactly the kind of drift doctor exists to surface
  - `.balls/tasks` symlink exists and resolves to
    `state-repo/.balls/tasks` (bl-38dd's invariant; siblings
    bl-773e covers stale-target on remaster)

# Scope guardrail

Read-only, as doctor always is. `repair` stays the only action verb
(`auto_provision_master` handles the materialize-on-discover path;
doctor just reports). Per the user's standing memory: `repair` is the
only action verb in this surface.

# Origin

Flagged during bl-38dd review — the symlink fix moved the goalposts
on what 'healthy master_url repo' means, and doctor needs to catch up.
title: bl doctor: learn .balls/state-repo / master_url layout
